Package org.areslib.subsystems

Class AresSuperstructure<S extends Enum<S>>

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.command.SubsystemBase
org.areslib.subsystems.AresSuperstructure<S>
Type Parameters:
S - The enum type representing superstructure states.
All Implemented Interfaces:
Subsystem
public abstract class AresSuperstructure<S extends Enum<S>> extends SubsystemBase
Base class for a robot Superstructure.

A Superstructure is a "meta-subsystem" that coordinates multiple smaller subsystems (Intake, Arm, Shooter) into high-level logical states. It uses an internal StateMachine to ensure transition safety and deterministic behavior.

    • stateMachine

      protected final StateMachine<S extends Enum<S>> stateMachine

  • Constructor Details

    • AresSuperstructure

      public AresSuperstructure(String name, Class<S> enumClass, S initialState, Supplier<Pose2d> poseSupplier)
      Constructs the superstructure.
      Parameters:
      name - Name for telemetry keys.
      enumClass - The state enum class.
      initialState - The state to start in.
      poseSupplier - Supplier for robot field pose.

  • Method Details

    • requestState

      public Command requestState(S target)
      Request a transition to a new state. Transition will be rejected if illegal in the state machine transition table.
      Parameters:
      target - The desired state.
      Returns:
      A command to perform the request.

    • forceState

      public void forceState(S target)
      Force the superstructure into a state, bypassing transition table logic.
      Parameters:
      target - The state to force.

    • periodic

      public void periodic()
      Description copied from interface: Subsystem
      This method is called periodically by the CommandScheduler. Useful for updating subsystem-specific state that you don't want to defer to a Command.

    • updateMechanisms

      protected abstract void updateMechanisms(S state)
      Subclasses implement this to drive subsystem targets based on the current superstructure state.
      Parameters:
      state - The current active state.

    • getState

      public S getState()
